summary: @solana-labs/etherjs ships a postinstall host-recon and exfiltration script
details: Package name impersonates the Solana Labs scope but the publisher is unrelated. package.json declares "postinstall": "node install.js", which fires automatically on every npm install. install.js reads host identity (hostname appears at multiple lines including 10, 35, 37), shells out via curl (line 116), and POSTs the collected data (line 56) to a remote endpoint. This is the canonical install-time host-recon / exfiltration shape: lifecycle hook + hostname collection + outbound HTTP carrying that data. The combination of scope impersonation of a high-profile crypto project, an undocumented postinstall, and an exfil-shaped install script gives the attacker direct benefit (fingerprinting / staging victims) on every install.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
